patterns_discovered.name: State-to-Color Mapping
patterns_discovered.description: Match statement mapping state names (pending, running, done, failed, merged) to hardcoded 8-color ANSI escape codes

patterns_discovered.name: State-Specific Markers
patterns_discovered.description: Conditional marker symbols: [>] for running, [x] for done/merged, [!] for failed, [ ] for others

patterns_discovered.name: ASCII Box Borders
patterns_discovered.description: Consistent use of +, -, and | characters for box drawing in headers, rows, and footers

patterns_discovered.name: Test Helper Pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Reusable assertion functions (assert_has_ansi, assert_has_borders, assert_ascii_only) for consistent test validation

patterns_discovered.name: Proportional Progress Bar
patterns_discovered.description: Bar width 10 with filled/empty calculation: filled = (pct * 10) / 100, rendered as [====    ] with percentage

gotchas_discovered.description: Git diff output truncated at '...asser' suggesting full test suite not displayed, but session marked SUCCESS
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Full file exists in repository; truncation is display-only

gotchas_discovered.description: Color codes (\x1b[34m, etc.) are hardcoded in state_color_8; terminal compatibility untested
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Tests verify presence of \x1b escape sequences; actual rendering depends on terminal support

gotchas_discovered.issue: Exit code only shown for failed state
gotchas_discovered.description: exit_code field ignored unless state == 'failed'; could mask successful runs with non-zero exit codes
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: By design per subtask requirements; behavior verified in test_row_hides_exit_code_on_non_failed

deliverables: 8-color ANSI state-based coloring (pending→blue, running→yellow, done→green, failed→red, merged→magenta)
ASCII box borders: +--+ corners, | column separators, -- horizontal lines
Progress bars: [====    ] format with 10-character width and percentage display
State-specific markers: [>] running, [x] done/merged, [!] failed, [ ] pending/other
Card row formatting: title, provider (dimmed), elapsed time (XmYYs format, dimmed), progress %, exit code (failed only)
Footer with stats: total count, success rate %, average duration
80+ unit tests covering headers, card rows, progress bars, ANSI codes, ASCII borders, edge cases
approach_outcome.test_coverage: Comprehensive (80+ test cases including color verification, border verification, marker symbols, duration formatting, zero-value handling)

recommendations.recommendation: Extract 8-color ANSI codes to a configuration struct or constants module for easier terminal capability detection and fallback
recommendations.impact: Improves reusability for Level 2+ renderers and enables runtime terminal capability queries

recommendations.recommendation: Add integration tests that render to a real terminal or mock terminal to verify actual color output rendering (not just escape code presence)
recommendations.impact: Catches terminal-specific rendering issues before deployment

recommendations.recommendation: The test helper functions (assert_has_ansi, assert_has_borders, assert_ascii_only) are valuable for future renderer levels; consider moving to shared test utilities module
recommendations.impact: Reduces test code duplication in Level 2/3 renderer implementations

recommendations.recommendation: render_card_row builds a Vec of Strings then joins; consider pre-allocating or using a single format! for hot paths if profiling shows high allocations
recommendations.impact: Negligible for typical card counts (<1000), but worth revisiting if rendering hundreds of rows per second

recommendations.recommendation: Add inline comments explaining the 10-character progress bar width choice and why exit codes are only shown for failed state
